page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server provides 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled and dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highmem-48 demonstrates strong performance profiles. It achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing tests. Multi-core performance is also robust, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. The instance excels at low-latency operations, delivering 58.32 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 73.99 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the integrated AMX instructions enable strong prompt processing and text generation speeds for small and medium LLM models, though performance drops to the bottom 25% when executing text generation on large 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

This instance offers high resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or discrete GPU acceleration. The combination of 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU and dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources makes it highly suitable for enterprise databases, memory-cached web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 48 vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM, delivering 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 24 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache, this x86_64 instance features advanced hardware acceleration including Intel AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for single-core processing, image manipulation, and text processing. It also shows strong multi-core performance in Passmark and Geekbench tests, alongside a high static web serving throughput of 73.99 GB/sec. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it represents a resource-dense, qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads. The c4-highmem-48 is highly suited for enterprise databases, in-memory caching, software compilation, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs and 372 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon virtual machine offering high memory density and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmark performance
Strong multi-core capabilities with 17,983 Geekbench and 57,593 Passmark scores
Well-suited for enterprise databases, in-memory caching, and medium-scale LLM inference
